suggestions.title: Break the JobsBitsMonitor cycle
suggestions.description: Today's `PodspecDependencies.md` still shows two cycles: `JobsBitsMonitor -> JobsNetWorkTools -> JobsBitsMonitor` and `JobsBitsMonitor -> JobsNetWorkTools -> JobsOCTools -> JobsBitsMonitor`. Those podspecs are all part of the fresh local-pod migration, so cutting one edge is a high-leverage compile unblock now.
suggestions.prompt: Trace the `JobsBitsMonitor -> JobsNetWorkTools -> JobsOCTools` podspec cycle, identify the minimal dependency edge to remove or split, and prepare the code changes needed to break the cycle without changing runtime behavior.

suggestions.title: Replace remaining JobsByOCPods umbrella imports
suggestions.description: There are still many local pod headers importing `<JobsByOCPods/UIKits.h>`, including files under `JobsOCTools`, `JobsAPIs`, `JobsSuspend`, `JobsFiltrationView`, and `JobsBitsMonitor`. That matches the recent import-cleanup commits and is a likely remaining source of missing-header fallout after the pod split.
suggestions.prompt: Find the remaining local pod headers that still import `<JobsByOCPods/UIKits.h>`, group them by owning pod, and change the first batch to use the narrow pod/module imports their podspecs actually declare.

suggestions.title: Harden ImportPatch example exclusions
suggestions.description: The new import-rewrite step in `Podfile` only skips `/Example/` and `/Demo/`, but `ManualByOCPods@Pods` still has 35 duplicate header names from lower-case example trees, including 32 copies of `AppDelegate.h`. Tightening that filter should make the rewrite deterministic for the manual local pods.
suggestions.prompt: Inspect `patch_development_pod_imports_by_generated_headers` in `Podfile`, confirm which duplicate headers are coming from lower-case example/sample folders, and update the script so those folders stop polluting the generated import rewrite.

suggestions.title: Clear the stale manifest lock blocker
suggestions.description: The only concrete build failure visible in `.codex-derived-data` is `[CP] Check Pods Manifest.lock`, but `Podfile.lock` and `Pods/Manifest.lock` currently diff clean. That makes this a likely stale build-artifact blocker hiding the next real compile error.
suggestions.prompt: Compare the current `Podfile.lock`, `Pods/Manifest.lock`, and the cached `.codex-derived-data` build scripts, explain why Xcode is still tripping `[CP] Check Pods Manifest.lock`, and get me to the next real compile error without changing source code yet.

suggestions.title: Finish the JobsRichTextUtils extraction
suggestions.description: Your uncommitted work already flips `JobsRichTextUtils` off `JobsBaseUI`, adds `JobsBaseUI -> JobsRichTextUtils`, and updates `WHToastExtra` and `JobsTimeUtils` dependencies in `Podfile.lock`. A focused audit of that seam is the most concrete code path to surface the next missing import or podspec declaration from the local-pod split.
suggestions.prompt: Audit the current `JobsRichTextUtils` extraction from `JobsBaseUI`: verify the changed podspecs and headers (`JobsRichTextUtils`, `JobsBaseUI`, `WHToastExtra`, `JobsTimeUtils`) are internally consistent, identify the next compile-breaking import/dependency mismatch on that seam, and prepare the minimal code changes needed to fix it.

suggestions.title: 修复 JobsByOCPods 缺失 JobsModel 导致的首个编译阻塞
